title: What are your most absurd algos? See mine in the post..
author: literally_joe_bauers
selftext:
Since being able to use AI for coding I 10x my code output (I am still slow as I tend to micromanage the AI code..) but that gave me the opportunity to implement some of the more absurd „algos“ I am familiar with and test them for their (explorative) potentials.. do you do similar? If yes, what was the most absurd algo that you tried (and failed? :D)

Here is my top 3:

3) Epsilon-Machine (works somewhat, but I would need to invest a lot more work)

2) Barnes-Hut (works, but I do not yet know how good)

1) Genetic Algorithms (e.g. CMA) (works for policies)

Cheers!

comments.author: LiveBeyondNow
comments.body: I have 2 which are near production (and testing well), and 2 others that may never see a dollar but not sure they’re absurd. I’d say ones more run-o-the-mill, and the other a bit more esoteric. The other two are quirky, but 3 of the 4 are mean reversion, another is hybrid’ish (some trend following).

comments.author: Amberkhell
comments.body: Try SmartEdge Trading and pray for me.
